payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the current, more specific, or more recent memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered out because the system identified it as superseded, meaning it should no longer be treated as the authoritative version of that fact.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new because it appears to be the newer, current memory for Sam from Goldman, while mem_old was excluded because it is marked or inferred to be superseded by the newer record.

entity_resolution.reason: The recall favors the newer memory record (mem_new) for Sam from Goldman, while the older record (mem_old) is treated as superseded and therefore filtered out.

payload.intent: Explain a recall decision involving Sam from Goldman, including why mem_new was returned and mem_old was filtered out as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: Sam is associated with Goldman in the recalled context, and mem_new is the preferred current memory for this fact.
mem_old contains an older version of the Sam-from-Goldman fact and should not be treated as current.

details: mem_new selected as current recall result
No durable ambiguity removed beyond the superseded/current distinction

repair_options: Keep mem_new as the active memory and mark mem_old as superseded.
If both records are meant to coexist, add a timestamp or version field to clarify which one is current.
If the supersession is incorrect, re-open the conflict and verify whether mem_old still contains unique unsaved information.
